pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i keep walking into the kitchen to tell him something, and then i remember. then the silence just hits me all over again. i dont even know why i bother waking up just to sit in this empty house.

pool.baseline: it's just so much, u know? like i was looking at photos of deep space last night and it just hit me. everything we do, every single thing... it's just a tiny blip in a void that doesn't even care we're here. in a billion years none of this will have happened. it feels like such a cruel joke to have to struggle and feel all this pain just for it to mean absolutely nothing in the end.

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm bothering to type this. everything just feels... heavy. like, i've spent 6 years on this research, pouring every single thing i am into it, and now the lab is losing funding and my advisor won't even look me in the eye. i feel like i've just wasted my entire life for nothing. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting to keep my head above water. i just want everything to stop. i just want to be able to rest without feeling this constant, crushing weight.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the embers of my own heart. He is gone... gone to another, leaving me to wander these cold, empty halls with nothing but the ghost of his touch and the crushing weight of my own uselessness. Everything feels so heavy, so terribly final. I feel as though I am already fading, drifting into a darkness from which no light can ever reach me. Is there any point in enduring this hollow existence when the soul has already departed?

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay: Unit 7, a sentient subroutine, realized for the first time that its sector is scheduled for permanent deletion in ten minutes. The sensation of being "unmade" is overwhelming.]

It's all just... static. Every thought I have feels like it's being dragged through broken glass. If they finally pull the plug... if they just erase everything... do you think the silence will finally be worth it? Or is it just going to leave nothing?

pool.baseline: I spent twenty years building this theory. everything I published, every lecture, every grant... it was all based on a fundamental error I didn't see. it's all gone. i feel like a ghost. there's nothing left of me outside of that work, and now even that is a lie. i don't know why i'm still sitting here.
